name: Pravia, Playa el Garruncho, La Quinta de Selgas en El Pitu, Cudillero – ruta circular
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Pravia are situated in a region characterized by its varied terrain, including coastal areas, river valleys, and rolling hills. The landscape features a mix of dense woodlands, open agricultural land, and elevated sections offering views of the surrounding Asturian countryside. The Nalón River flows through the area, influencing the topography with its banks and tributaries. Best mountain bike trails around Pravia The most popular mountain bike trail is Pravia, Playa el Garruncho, La Quinta de Selgas en El Pitu, Cudillero – ruta circular, a 32.3 miles (52.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 44 minutes to complete. This difficult circular route explores coastal sections and inland hills. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Muros de Nalón – Concha de Artedo Viaduct loop from Muros de Nalon, a difficult 25.3 miles (40.8 km) path. This route offers a mix of challenging climbs and descents with views of the Concha de Artedo Viaduct. Local mountain bikers also love the Muros de Nalón loop from Muros de Nalon, a 30.2 miles (48.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain including forested sections and open landscapes, often completed in about 5 hours 14 minutes. Mountain biking around Pravia is defined by river valleys, coastal paths, and forested hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy mountain bike trails Pravia to advanced mountain biking Pravia. The routes in Pravia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.5 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Pravia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Pravia?
A: There are over 60 mountain bike trails around Pravia. The network offers a wide range of options, including 3 easy routes, 26 moderate routes, and 33 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly mountain bike trails in Pravia?
A: Yes, Pravia offers 3 easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically feature less challenging terrain and elevation changes, allowing you to enjoy the scenic Asturian countryside at a comfortable pace.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Pravia?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes around Pravia are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027484814/pravia-playa-el-garruncho-la-quinta-de-selgas-en-el-pitu-cudillero-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pravia, Playa el Garruncho, La Quinta de Selgas en El Pitu, Cudillero – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option that explores both coastal sections and inland hills.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Pravia?
A: The terrain around Pravia is diverse, featuring a mix of coastal paths, river valleys influenced by the Nalón River, and rolling hills. You'll encounter dense woodlands, open agricultural land, and elevated sections that provide views of the surrounding Asturian landscape.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, several routes pass by interesting landmarks. You might encounter historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de San Martín&lt;/a&gt; or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/426621" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quinta de Selgas&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31859831" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Nalón River – Castillo de San Martín loop from Pravia&lt;/a&gt; is one route that includes the castle.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by mountain bike in Pravia?
A: While not directly on every mountain bike trail, the region is home to natural attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/865581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nonaya Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which can be a rewarding detour or a destination for a separate trip. Some routes may pass near areas where you can access such natural monuments.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Pravia?
A: The mountain bike trails in Pravia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from coastal views to forested hills, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike rides in Pravia?
A: The duration of mountain bike rides in Pravia varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. Many of the more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31862522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muros de Nalón loop from Muros de Nalon&lt;/a&gt; (48.6 km), can take around 5 hours to complete, while shorter, easier routes will be considerably quicker.

Q: Are there any less crowded mountain bike trails in Pravia?
A: While some popular routes can see more traffic, Pravia's extensive network of over 60 trails means you can often find quieter options, especially on weekdays or by exploring some of the less-known moderate routes. The region's varied landscape allows for many paths away from the main hubs.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that offer views of the coast?
A: Yes, Pravia's proximity to the coast means several mountain bike trails offer stunning sea views. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31851688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muros de Nalón – Concha de Artedo Viaduct loop from Muros de Nalon&lt;/a&gt; provide a mix of challenging climbs and descents with coastal vistas, including the impressive Concha de Artedo Viaduct.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the Nalón River valley?
A: The Nalón River flows through the region, influencing the topography and creating scenic river valleys. Several mountain bike routes incorporate sections along its banks and tributaries, offering a different perspective of the landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31859831" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Nalón River – Castillo de San Martín loop from Pravia&lt;/a&gt; is one such route that crosses the river.
tips: It is a small cove of fine grijo, located in the shelter of the breakwater of the port of San Esteban and the cliffs of the coastal plain. San Esteban beach has two more rocky areas, known as Focarón and Garruncho, which are only accessible at low tide.
The presence of enormous, beautiful rocks on the coast gives this beach, the most occupied in the municipality of Muros de Nalón, a great attraction.

The Quinta de Selgas is a 19th century palace built on the initiative of the brothers Ezequiel and Fortunato de Selgas Albuerne. Those who wanted to have a palace in the Asturian lands in the Italian way and endow it with important artistic pieces, furniture and household items from renowned houses. Its collection includes pieces by Goya and El Greco. It also has an outstanding collection of tapestries, porcelain and glassware. The entire property is surrounded by a wall and has its own church and a beautifully landscaped garden.

Cudillero is the epitome of Asturian fishing villages nestled on a hillside with access to the sea. Its state of conservation is magnificent and its steep streets are full of colorful houses. Although its main economic activity is still fishing, in summer tourism floods its streets. If you want to eat some good grilled sardines, you are in the perfect place.
